# macro-editor

> A fast terminal text editor written in Rust — a `micro` replacement with integrated file tree, syntax highlighting, and LSP support.

## Project

- Binary: `macro`
- Language: Rust (edition 2021)
- TUI: ratatui + crossterm
- Syntax highlighting: syntect (pure Rust, `default-fancy` feature, no C deps)
- Config: `~/.config/macro/config.toml` (TOML, serde)
- Packaging: RPM spec at `packaging/macro-editor.spec` (Fedora COPR)

## Key features

- Integrated file tree (left panel, toggleable)
- Incremental syntax highlighting (recomputes only from the edited line)
- Multi-tab editing (up to 8 open files)
- Mouse support: click, scroll, drag selection
- LSP autocompletion (stdio JSON-RPC, auto-triggered while typing)
- Keyboard selection (Shift+arrows, Ctrl+A), clipboard (Ctrl+C/X/V)
- Line numbers, configurable tab size and theme

## Source layout

- `src/main.rs` — entry point, CLI args
- `src/app.rs` — event loop, keyboard + mouse, LSP glue
- `src/editor.rs` — text buffer + 29 unit tests
- `src/ui.rs` — ratatui rendering
- `src/highlight.rs` — syntect wrapper, HighlightCache
- `src/file_tree.rs` — file tree widget
- `src/config.rs` — config
- `src/lsp.rs` — LSP client

## Build

```sh
cargo build --release
cargo test
```

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/firexrwt/macro-code-editor
- Agent guide: AGENTS.md
- Contributing: CONTRIBUTING.md
